name: Organization Management
description: Create, list, view, update, and delete organizations; invite members; assign/update member roles; view org-scoped scan history and dashboard.

frontend.evidence: MOSTLY FIXED 2026-07-09, completed 2026-07-11. Added a new #page-orgs (list view + detail view: dashboard stats, members table, settings, danger zone) plus 4 modals (create/invite/remove-confirm/delete-confirm) to frontend/user-dashboard.html, wired to the existing, unmodified backend. Confirmed before building that no near-miss page existed under frontend/enterprise*.html (frontend/enterprise-dashboard.html is a threat-intel feed, frontend/enterprise-portal.html is SSO/SIEM integration docs, frontend/enterprise-kpi-dashboard.html is CDB's own internal revenue analytics, frontend/enterprise-security.html is marketing copy — none call any /api/orgs route), so this was a clean build with no duplication. 2026-07-11: the deliberately-deferred 10th handler, handleOrgScans (org-wide paginated scan history across every active member), is now wired too — a new 'Scan History' card in the detail view (module filter + Prev/Next pager) calling GET /api/orgs/:id/scans, real field names (module/target_summary/risk_score/scanned_by/scanned_at). Found and fixed in the same pass: the handler's own `total` field was `results?.length` (i.e. capped at whatever `limit` was requested, never the real row count) — a pager built on it would work on page 1 and then be unable to tell 'more pages exist' from 'this is everything' the moment an org had more scans than one page; fixed with a real `COUNT(*)` over the same WHERE clause (member ids + optional module filter), zero change to the SELECT query's own shape. All 10 of 10 backend handlers now have a real UI consumer.

navigation.evidence: New 'Organizations' nav-item in a new 'Team' sidebar section (frontend/user-dashboard.html), visible to every signed-in customer regardless of whether they currently belong to an org — the list view's empty state offers 'Create Organization' directly, since a brand-new signup account has zero orgs and there was previously no discoverable path to create one at all.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/orgRbacIsolation.test.mjs and workers/test/phase9OrgDashboardSchema.test.mjs (backend, pre-existing, unchanged) plus workers/test/userDashboardOrgManagement.test.mjs (2026-07-09, 17 tests) — cross-checks the new frontend against workers/src/index.js and workers/src/handlers/orgManagement.js directly: every API call uses the real route/method/field names (including the org_id-vs-id and slug-vs-UUID gotchas the backend contract has), and the client-side RBAC surfaced in the UI (who sees Invite/Settings/Danger-Zone, who gets a role dropdown, who can remove/leave) matches the backend's actual enforcement exactly, not just visually. Independently re-verified live via a real headless-Chromium Playwright session — 27/27 checks across 3 scenarios: full OWNER lifecycle (empty state → create → detail view with live dashboard stats → invite → change a member's role → remove a member → save settings → back to list), and a dedicated MEMBER-role permission-boundary check confirming a non-owner sees zero management controls and only 'Leave' (never 'Remove') on their own row. axe-core WCAG2A/AA scan found and fixed two real new issues (a critical 'label' violation on the settings form's Name field — added proper for/id pairing rather than the weaker placeholder-as-fallback pattern used elsewhere in this file — and a critical 'select-name' violation on the dynamically-generated per-member role dropdown — added aria-label); remaining flagged violations are the same pre-existing, site-wide '--muted'/'.badge-gray'/'.btn-danger' color-contrast issue already documented as out-of-scope on CAP-IDN-002 and CAP-RBAC-002. 2026-07-11: new workers/test/orgScansPagination.test.mjs (8 tests) for the handleOrgScans wiring — backend tests seed 28 scan_history rows (more than the 20-row default page size) to make the total-count bug and its fix diverge (a 2-row fixture can't distinguish them), confirm page-2 math, module-filter narrowing, and both a non-member and a real member of a DIFFERENT org correctly 403 (no cross-org leak); frontend static tests confirm the real field names; a real headless-Chromium session against a minimal DOM harness with the real extracted loadOrgScans()/orgScansPage() functions (not a reimplementation) drove a full page1 → next → prev → module-filter → empty-state sequence — 28 rows across 2 pages, range text, and Prev/Next disabled-state all correct at every step, zero page errors.

verification.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-09 (own commit, own regression tests). workers/src/handlers/orgManagement.js re-read in full to confirm every request/response field name and RBAC rule before writing any UI code (org_id from POST /api/orgs vs id from GET /api/orgs/:id; GET /api/orgs/:id resolves slug-or-id but GET /api/orgs/:id/dashboard requires the real UUID; the documented zero-member dashboard response shape with no summary key). Dynamic: real headless-Chromium Playwright session against the changed file served locally, mocking the full backend contract per RBAC role (OWNER and MEMBER) — 27/27 checks. Full backend suite green: 183 files / 1944 tests (182/1927 baseline + 1 new file/17 tests). scripts/seo-structure-lock.mjs: all 22 pages green (unaffected). 2026-07-11: handleOrgScans wired (see frontend.evidence for the total-count bug fixed alongside it) — workers/src/index.js's route registration re-read to confirm the real path/method; a real headless-Chromium session against a minimal DOM harness with the actual extracted loadOrgScans()/orgScansPage() functions drove a full pagination sequence (28 seeded rows, page1 → next → prev → module-filter → empty-state), all correct, zero page errors. Full backend suite 211 files/2188 tests green. customer_journey_complete stays false and operational_status is PILOT ONLY (not GA) pending a real dynamic_browser verification pass against LIVE production specifically (this pass verified against a local harness/mocked backend, not the deployed site) — not because any shipped part is in doubt.

contradicts_doc.claim: Organizations: create / members / RBAC / dashboard / scan history | GA APPROVED | Full admin loop live-verified this phase; RC-B1 fix verified at Phase IX gate
contradicts_doc.correction: The original "live-verified" evidence behind this claim was scripts/ceap-sweep.mjs, which calls the API directly (dynamic_api) — no dynamic_browser verification existed, and no frontend page called any /api/orgs* endpoint at all, until the 2026-07-09 fix. A real UI now exists for all 10 backend handlers, including org-wide scan history as of 2026-07-11 — but no production dynamic_browser pass has been performed for any of it yet, so 'GA APPROVED... live-verified' remains inaccurate specifically on the verification-method claim, not on functional completeness.

notes: Was the highest-value single gap identified across the whole platform: full, working, RBAC-enforced backend with a complete admin loop, and precisely zero customer-facing UI. Fixed as a minimally-disruptive, additive build: zero changes to the existing, working orgManagement.js backend; a new sidebar section and page reusing the dashboard's exact existing markup/CSS/JS conventions (form-group/modal/card/table patterns) rather than introducing a new design language. Client-side permission checks were derived directly from the backend's own enforcement code (read first, then mirrored), not guessed at, and independently regression-tested against both. 2026-07-11: the one deliberately-deferred increment — org-wide scan history (handleOrgScans) — is now also wired, closing the last of the 10 handlers. Found a genuine backend bug while wiring it (total was page-size, not a real count) and fixed it in the same pass rather than building a pager against data that would silently lie past page 1 — see frontend.evidence. Remaining gap, unchanged from before: no production dynamic_browser verification pass has been run for any part of org management yet (this and the 2026-07-09 build were both verified against local/mocked backends only) — that, not any known functional defect, is what's keeping operational_status at PILOT ONLY.

name: Organization Activity Log (Audit Trail)
description: OWNER/ADMIN-visible audit trail of org membership and settings changes (invites, role changes, removals, org updates/deletion) — the customer-facing counterpart to the platform staff's own merged audit log (CAP-ADMIN-001).

frontend.evidence: ADDED 2026-07-11 (competitor-parity wave). New 'Activity Log' card in the existing org detail view (#org-audit-card), positioned between Settings and Danger Zone, visible only to OWNER/ADMIN (same canManage gate as #org-settings-card). loadOrgAudit() calls the new GET /api/orgs/:id/audit endpoint and renders actor name, a human-readable action label (ORG_AUDIT_LABELS), and a per-action detail string (orgAuditDetail()), all HTML-escaped via the existing orgEsc() helper.

navigation.evidence: Embedded in the already-discoverable Organizations page (CAP-ORG-001) — no new nav entry needed, loads automatically when an OWNER/ADMIN opens their org's detail view.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/orgAuditLog.test.mjs (17 tests, new): real in-memory SQLite (node:sqlite, same convention as workers/test/orgRbacIsolation.test.mjs) proving audit rows are written on invite/role-change/remove/leave/update/delete with correct actor+metadata; audit writing degrades gracefully (never blocks the underlying action) if audit_log is unavailable; the read endpoint is OWNER/ADMIN-only (plain MEMBER, a non-member outsider, and a real OWNER of a DIFFERENT org all correctly get 403 — no cross-org existence leak); route-registration contract check; frontend static-parse checks confirming the Activity Log card's visibility gate, data loading, and escaped rendering.

verification.evidence: Backend: 17/17 tests passing against a real SQLite engine executing the real handler code. Frontend: real headless-Chromium Playwright session against the changed frontend/user-dashboard.html, hitting the REAL live backend for signup/org-create (routes that already exist in production) and a locally-mocked response — shaped field-for-field to match handleGetOrgAuditLog's real, tested output — for the one new route that cannot exist on production until this PR merges and deploys. Confirmed: Activity Log card visible and correctly populated for the OWNER with human-readable labels and per-action detail text, zero JS errors. customer_journey_complete stays false pending a post-merge production pass once the real endpoint is live (same discipline as every other capability in this registry — dynamic_api/dynamic_browser against a route that doesn't exist yet pre-merge is a contradiction in terms, not a shortcut taken here).

notes: Found via a structured competitor comparison (CrowdStrike, SentinelOne, Palo Alto Cortex, Recorded Future, Google Mandiant, ThreatConnect): 4 of 6 vendors publicly document some form of customer-admin-visible audit trail (ThreatConnect's is the most granular — actor/action/timestamp/source IP/session ID). This platform had a staff-only equivalent (CAP-ADMIN-001) but nothing an org OWNER could see for their own org. Reuses the existing, already-migrated D1 audit_log table (the same one aiSecurityCopilot.js's writeCopilotAuditLog() and the SSO handler already write to, and handleAdminAudit already merges into its staff view) — zero new schema, zero migration dependency, unlike the Threat Graph findings-persistence fix (PR #159) which needed a genuinely new column. See docs/capability-registry/PROGRAM_BOARD.md's 2026-07-11 competitor-gap-analysis session log entry for the full comparison and the larger, explicitly-scoped-out gaps (pervasive org-RBAC enforcement outside this one page, in-product support ticketing, enterprise SSO self-service configuration, API-key system consolidation).
